heading: Reserves: duty with or without pay
label: 12315 Reserves: duty with or without pay
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)683(a)683(b)50:971.50:972., §§ 240, 241, .July 9, 1952, ch. 60866 Stat. 492In subsection (a), the word “title” is substituted for the word “chapter”. The provisions of this title relating to active duty of reservists are based on the Armed Forces Reserve Act of 1952. The words “shall be considered * * * as if it were” are substituted for the words “shall be counted * * * the same as like”.In subsections (a) and (b), the words “active duty for training” are omitted as covered by the words “active duty”.In subsection (b), the word “kept” is substituted for the words “retained or continued”. The words “pursuant to law” are omitted as surplusage.
Amendments1994— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337section 683 of this title

children.text: with the pay and allowances provided by law; or

children.label: b
children.text: A Reserve who is kept on active duty after his term of service expires is entitled to pay and allowances while on that duty, except as they may be forfeited under the approved sentence of a court-martial or by non-judicial punishment by a commanding officer or when he is otherwise in a non-pay status.
